name: Bitcoin
one_line_thesis: The base monetary asset for the crypto stack keeps compounding as trust in the network, liquidity, and institutional plumbing deepen.
why_now: Bitcoin is now close enough to institutions to matter, but still early enough that second-order businesses and infrastructure around it can re-rate fast.
catalysts: Institutional adoption and treasury accumulation broaden the buyer base.
Settlement demand increases as more financial infrastructure is built on top of Bitcoin rails.
Macro stress can reinforce the demand for an apolitical reserve asset.
red_flags: Narrative can outrun real transactional utility in some cycles.
Custody concentration and regulatory bottlenecks can slow adoption.
Mining economics remain sensitive to energy and policy shifts.
diligence_questions: Is the security budget still durable through the next halving cycle?
Where is non-speculative transaction demand actually growing?
Which public companies are creating the best Bitcoin-adjacent picks-and-shovels exposure?

leadership_resilience.evidence: Founder absence turned the asset into a durable social and technical coordination layer.

mission_clarity.evidence: Simple monetary positioning remains legible even after multiple market cycles.

moat.evidence: The liquidity, brand, security, and Lindy effect remain difficult to replicate.

distribution.evidence: Exchange access, custody, ETFs, and treasury adoption expand global reach.

market_tailwind.evidence: Demand for non-sovereign assets can grow if fiat distrust and digital-native wealth continue to compound.

financial_strength.evidence: The network has deep liquidity and no corporate balance sheet dependency.

regulatory_resilience.evidence: The asset is more accepted than most crypto assets, but mining, custody, and tax rules can still shift.

strategic_optionality.evidence: The asset benefits from financialization even if application layers remain elsewhere.

timing.evidence: Adoption is more mature than earlier cycles, so upside depends on institutional depth rather than novelty alone.

name: Coinbase
one_line_thesis: Coinbase can win as the regulated distribution and custody layer for the U.S. crypto economy if it keeps converting policy clarity into product breadth.
why_now: It sits at the intersection of regulation, custody, consumer access, and developer rails, which can create multiple monetization surfaces as the asset class matures.
catalysts: More assets, wallets, and onchain services can increase revenue mix beyond spot trading.
Policy clarity in the United States could lift product velocity and margins.
Institutional custody and stablecoin economics can compound alongside retail activity.
red_flags: Revenue still moves with trading activity and market sentiment.
Regulatory outcomes can sharply change product availability.
Fee compression is a persistent long-term threat.
diligence_questions: How diversified is gross profit away from transaction revenue?
Which products have the best odds of becoming indispensable infrastructure?
Does policy progress actually expand moat or simply commoditize access?

leadership_resilience.evidence: Armstrong has shown cycle endurance and willingness to build through hostile regulatory periods.

mission_clarity.evidence: The company has remained focused on making crypto easier to access and integrate.

moat.evidence: Brand trust, compliance, and institutional rails form a meaningful moat in the United States.

distribution.evidence: The platform is a default entry point for retail, institutions, and many developers.

market_tailwind.evidence: Crypto market maturation expands custody, staking, payments, and onchain utility.

financial_strength.evidence: Scale and balance sheet flexibility are stronger than most crypto-native peers.

regulatory_resilience.evidence: Regulation can help Coinbase, but the path is still adversarial and uneven.

strategic_optionality.evidence: The business has multiple expansion paths across custody, wallets, payments, and developer platforms.

timing.evidence: The market already recognizes the franchise, but structural policy wins could still move the narrative.

name: Bittensor
one_line_thesis: Bittensor is a higher-volatility bet on decentralized AI infrastructure where ecosystem quality and validator economics matter more than headline hype.
why_now: It sits in the overlap of AI, crypto incentives, and open networks, which can attract outsized attention when usage catches the story.
catalysts: More useful subnets can create real demand for the network rather than purely reflexive token demand.
Institutional validators and infrastructure partners can legitimize the ecosystem.
AI cost pressure may increase interest in open, incentive-driven compute and model networks.
red_flags: AI narratives attract speculative capital faster than durable usage.
Network complexity can make it hard to separate signal from noise.
Token economics and governance quality need ongoing scrutiny.
diligence_questions: Which subnets are producing measurable user value today?
What part of the ecosystem is durable without token incentives?
How concentrated are validator power and token ownership?

leadership_resilience.evidence: Founders stayed committed to a difficult thesis before the AI market paid attention.

mission_clarity.evidence: The goal of an open market for machine intelligence is clear, even if execution is complex.

moat.evidence: Network design and early ecosystem mindshare matter, but the moat is still forming.

distribution.evidence: The ecosystem is still niche compared with mainstream AI platforms.

market_tailwind.evidence: AI demand and dissatisfaction with centralized control both create interest.

financial_strength.evidence: Economic durability depends on sustained subnet value rather than traditional cash flow.

regulatory_resilience.evidence: AI and token regulation can both create friction.

strategic_optionality.evidence: The protocol can expand through new subnets, infrastructure, and AI-native applications.

timing.evidence: It still feels early enough for a real edge if usage, not just price, keeps improving.

name: Zcash
one_line_thesis: Zcash is a mission-heavy privacy bet where technical credibility remains high but adoption and regulation are the gating variables.
why_now: If digital privacy becomes more valuable again, the protocol has real technical history; if not, it risks remaining intellectually strong but economically weak.
catalysts: Privacy-preserving payment demand could strengthen if surveillance concerns intensify.
Wallet and usability improvements can narrow the gap between technology and adoption.
Clearer compliance patterns could reopen exchange and institutional support.
red_flags: Regulatory hostility toward privacy tools can suppress distribution.
The network risks strong technology with weak commercial pull.
Competing privacy approaches may absorb demand before Zcash does.
diligence_questions: Is shielded usage actually growing in a durable way?
Can Zcash become easy enough for mainstream use without losing its edge?
How do governance and funding keep development sustainable?

leadership_resilience.evidence: The founding mission has been unusually consistent through long periods of low market enthusiasm.

mission_clarity.evidence: Private digital cash is a clear and coherent mission.

moat.evidence: The protocol has deep technical roots in privacy-preserving cryptography.

distribution.evidence: Exchange support and mainstream wallet support remain constrained.

market_tailwind.evidence: Privacy demand is real, but often politically and commercially constrained.

financial_strength.evidence: Sustainable development depends on governance and continuing ecosystem funding.

regulatory_resilience.evidence: Privacy-focused assets carry heavier compliance and listing risk.

strategic_optionality.evidence: The core technology can matter in broader privacy infrastructure, but monetization paths are narrow.

timing.evidence: The narrative can return, but there is no guarantee the market rewards it soon.

name: NEAR Protocol
one_line_thesis: NEAR is a platform bet on usable developer infrastructure, distribution, and AI-adjacent positioning rather than pure chain tribalism.
why_now: The chain competes in a crowded field, but founder quality and product emphasis give it optionality if developer and user experience wins matter more.
catalysts: Developer growth and chain abstraction tooling can make the platform easier to adopt.
AI-linked narratives can pull attention if they translate into actual product usage.
Consumer-grade applications could matter more if onboarding friction keeps falling.
red_flags: Layer-1 competition is saturated and capital-intensive.
Narrative pivots can dilute strategic focus.
Token-holder incentives do not guarantee durable app traction.
diligence_questions: Which apps on NEAR have real retention rather than temporary incentives?
How differentiated is the technology versus other general-purpose chains?
Do developer tools create a compounding advantage or just parity?

leadership_resilience.evidence: The founders combine serious technical depth with a long-term product-building posture.

mission_clarity.evidence: The pitch around usability and developer experience remains coherent.

moat.evidence: Technology and tooling are solid, though differentiation is hard in a crowded market.

distribution.evidence: Awareness exists, but user capture and developer mindshare are still uneven.

market_tailwind.evidence: If consumer crypto usage expands, clean onboarding matters.

financial_strength.evidence: Ecosystem durability depends on continued app growth rather than just treasury runway.

regulatory_resilience.evidence: General crypto platform risk remains meaningful but not uniquely severe.

strategic_optionality.evidence: Cross-chain tooling, AI alignment, and consumer apps create several shots on goal.

timing.evidence: The platform still has room to surprise if execution keeps improving before the market fully cares.

name: Robinhood
one_line_thesis: Robinhood can become a consumer financial super-app if it keeps turning low-friction distribution into higher-value products without breaking trust.
why_now: The company already owns consumer attention in investing, and product expansion can move it beyond trading into a wider financial relationship.
catalysts: Higher wallet share per user through retirement, cash management, and advisory layers.
International and crypto product expansion can widen the monetization envelope.
A stronger market environment tends to amplify user engagement and asset gathering.
red_flags: Regulatory pressure can quickly constrain growth tactics.
Retail engagement is cyclical and mood-driven.
Trust can be damaged fast if product complexity outpaces communication.
diligence_questions: Is average revenue per funded account rising for the right reasons?
Which products can endure a cold market with lower trading activity?
How defensible is the consumer relationship versus larger incumbents?

leadership_resilience.evidence: Management survived severe public scrutiny and kept shipping into a tougher environment.

mission_clarity.evidence: The democratization-of-finance framing remains consistent and product-relevant.

moat.evidence: Brand, onboarding, and user behavior data matter, though they are not impossible to copy.

distribution.evidence: Robinhood's consumer reach is one of its clearest strengths.

market_tailwind.evidence: Retail participation and product expansion both create growth paths.

financial_strength.evidence: The platform is large enough to invest through weaker periods.

regulatory_resilience.evidence: The company operates under constant regulatory attention.

strategic_optionality.evidence: Banking, advisory, prediction markets, and crypto can all extend the model.

timing.evidence: The business benefits when engagement returns, but optimism can get priced quickly.

name: Tesla
one_line_thesis: Tesla is a founder-driven industrial platform bet where manufacturing scale, software, and energy optionality can matter more than near-term auto sentiment.
why_now: The company already has global scale, but the next leg depends on whether autonomy, robotics, and energy become real earnings pillars rather than narrative support.
catalysts: Vehicle software and autonomy improvements can shift the margin story.
Energy generation and storage can become a larger share of the business mix.
Manufacturing efficiency can amplify upside if demand and product refresh align.
red_flags: Execution and communication are heavily tied to one polarizing leader.
The stock often prices in future businesses before economics are proven.
Regulatory and competitive pressure remain persistent.
diligence_questions: How much of the autonomy thesis is measurable rather than aspirational?
Can energy and software become material enough to diversify the auto cyclicality?
What indicators show durable demand strength without relying on price cuts?

leadership_resilience.evidence: The company has repeatedly navigated manufacturing, capital, and demand crises.

mission_clarity.evidence: The mission around sustainable energy still anchors the broader platform story.

moat.evidence: Brand, manufacturing know-how, vertical integration, and data advantages remain substantial.

distribution.evidence: Global consumer awareness and direct distribution are already world-class.

market_tailwind.evidence: Electrification, grid buildout, and autonomy all create long-duration demand vectors.

financial_strength.evidence: Scale and cash generation give the company room to keep investing.

regulatory_resilience.evidence: The company is exposed to subsidy shifts, trade policy, and autonomy scrutiny.

strategic_optionality.evidence: Energy, autonomy, robotics, and software all offer credible expansion paths.

timing.evidence: The company is not early, but inflection points in autonomy or energy could still change the earnings profile.
